sections.paragraph:
πλατυσμός, οῦ, ὁ (πλατύς; Diosc. 5, 6 al.; LXX; Philo, Somn. 2, 36).

① state of increasing development, extension, enlargement, expansion (schol. on Pind., Eustath., Prooem. 4) of a congregation (w. δόξα) 1 Cl 3:1.

② state of being ample, amplitude, extent (2 Km 22:20; Ps 17:20; 118:45), in imagery of patience, which prospers through broad application Hm 5, 2, 3 (εὐθηνέω b).—DELG s.v. 1 πλατύς.

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Eustath Eustath , XII a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 823
